account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

search_signals.text:
Kalshi, a CFTC-regulated prediction market, filed this Rule 6.3 amendment on March 2 (effective Mar 17) to codify existing practice: If the main subject of a contract (a real person) dies before expiration, they can pause trading and settle at the last traded price before death—or before it was reasonably anticipated. Their committee decides if needed; it's final.

Context: Kalshi avoids contracts that pay out directly on death (ethical/perverse incentive reasons). The recent Khamenei "out of of

The Kalshi prediction market (as of Feb 27) gave Dems 82% odds for the House, matching recent generic ballot polls averaging Dem +4 to +5 (Nate Silver, RCP, NYT tracking as of Mar 3). 

Senate forecasts differ: the map favors GOP (defending just 2 competitive seats vs Dems' tougher ones). Consensus from Cook, Sabato, 270toWin, and Reuters gives Republicans ~75-85% chance to hold majority. No major polls show Dems winning it. 

Image accurate for House markets; full claim mixes terms and overstat

search_signals.text: @karlbakenor @OrganizerMemes prediction markets aren't decision desks. what warrants a "projection"? 95% yes? 99% yes? that can occur well before election day. it's nonsensical for polymarket/kalshi to "project" a winner.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: Kalshi is a CFTC-regulated prediction market founded in 2018 by Tarek Mansour (CEO) and Luana Lopes Lara. They met at MIT and worked in finance at Goldman Sachs, Citadel, and Bridgewater before starting it to let people trade directly on event outcomes. Backed by investors like Sequoia, a16z, and Paradigm; recently valued at $11B.

the best thing you can do post-launch is keep your chart alive. consistent buys, active trading, and real volume signal to dexscreener and https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU that people care. a dead chart = nobody scrolling past it.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: nice branding. the tokens that actually survive on https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU are the ones that keep their chart alive post-launch. most people launch then just wait for buyers — but consistent volume is what gets you picked up by dexscreener scanners and onto trending pages where the real eyes are. early momentum is everything on solana right now
